title: SEELE
wikitext:
[]: SEELE             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Mon Feb 16, 2026 12:14 am OK, this is a funny one. Although the show CLEARLY indicates that SEELE are human beings who communicate using distorted audio via holograph group calls (unlike the committee meetings, which are normal holograph calls) there seems to be a large fan contingent who believe that the leaders of SEELE are "monoliths." Surely the audio only calls (and Gendo appears this way talking to Misato, along with Futsuki by the way) are represented in a way to SUGGEST monoliths ala 2001. But no one is supposed to think they are physically "monoliths." It's a communications system that masks their appearances as well as their voices, right? Anyway, I started this thread to discuss SEELE overall. How much is clear from the show (or the movie) and what sorts of headcanon does everyone have to explain the organization? My impression is that they are a secret society going back centuries, but I can't say where in the show I got this information.

[]: Re: SEELE             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Feb 16, 2026 12:59 pm This is probably due to the fact that in Rebuild, they ARE the monoliths, and a lot of modern fans are coming from that.

[]: Re: SEELE             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Mon Feb 16, 2026 9:49 pm They sure did a lot of weird things in the rebuilds. I wasn't entirely clear on that, but at least in the later films the Professor appears to be turning containers or something to euthanize them or whatever. I figured they came to NERV and downloaded their minds into the containers or something, after near third impact. But that is off-topic. I find SEELE one of the more fascinating and creepy aspects of NGE. It's also one of the more fun things to think about. Like whether the secret dead Sea Scrolls have anything to do with the real ones and if so, what. Are we to imagine ancient mystics knew something about the angels?

[]: Re: SEELE             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Feb 18, 2026 10:15 am Seele are plainly human; they turn into LCL during 3rd Impact like everyone else. Though Keel seem to have significant cyborg enhancements. According to the classified information, when the Dead Sea Scrolls were discovered only the parts of little consequence were released to the public, everything relating to Adam, Lilith, Spears of Longinus and impacts, know as the "Secret Dead Sea Scrolls" was supressed. Upon the discovery of the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls the world controlling entity Seele returned to its nearly forgotten religious roots and essentially became a death cult.

[]: Re: SEELE             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Wed Feb 18, 2026 7:41 pm thewayneiac wrote: Seele are plainly human; they turn into LCL during 3rd Impact like everyone else. Though Keel seem to have significant cyborg enhancements. According to the classified information, when the Dead Sea Scrolls were discovered only the parts of little consequence were released to the public, everything relating to Adam, Lilith, Spears of Longinus and impacts, know as the "Secret Dead Sea Scrolls" was supressed. Upon the discovery of the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls the world controlling entity Seele returned to its nearly forgotten religious roots and essentially became a death cult.

[]: Re: SEELE             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Feb 19, 2026 10:54 am Darth Plato wrote: thewayneiac#946085 wrote: Seele are plainly human; they turn into LCL during 3rd Impact like everyone else. Though Keel seem to have significant cyborg enhancements. According to the classified information, when the Dead Sea Scrolls were discovered only the parts of little consequence were released to the public, everything relating to Adam, Lilith, Spears of Longinus and impacts, know as the "Secret Dead Sea Scrolls" was supressed. Upon the discovery of the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls the world controlling entity Seele returned to its nearly forgotten religious roots and essentially became a death cult.

[]: Re: SEELE             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Fri Feb 20, 2026 6:29 pm Thank you!

[]: SEELE             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Mon Feb 16, 2026 12:14 am OK, this is a funny one. Anyway, I started this thread to discuss SEELE overall. How much is clear from the show (or the movie) and what sorts of headcanon does everyone have to explain the organization? My impression is that they are a secret society going back centuries, but I can't say where in the show I got this information.
